steps.title: Identify what campaigns should avoid
steps.content: The passage argues that despite polling failures, campaigns should not take a certain action. The passage then explains why—polling has strategic value beyond prediction. The blank describes an action that would naturally result from viewing polling as unreliable, but shouldn't be taken.

steps.title: Choose the word for that action
steps.content: NEGLECT (to fail to give proper attention to; to disregard) names the action campaigns would naturally take in response to viewing polling as unreliable—stop relying on it. Wang argues against this because polling still helps identify where strategic efforts are most effective. The other three don't fit the sense of abandoning or disregarding: distort means twisting or misrepresenting (not about using or not using); supplement means adding more (opposite direction); and enact means making into law (unrelated to polling). That matches choice B—B is correct.
